heading: Easements for rights-of-way
label: 2668 Easements for rights-of-way
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2668(a)2668(b)43:931b (less 2d and 3d provisos of 1st sentence, and less last sentence).43:931b (2d proviso of 1st sentence)., ; (31st through 43d words), .July 24, 1946, ch. 596, § 760 Stat. 643Oct. 25, 1951, ch. 563, § 10165 Stat. 6412668(c)43:931b (3d proviso of 1st sentence).2668(d)43:931b (last sentence) [43:931b is made applicable to the Navy by 50:171–1 (16th through 21st words)].In subsection (a), the word “conditions” is omitted as covered by the word “terms”. The description of the persons covered in the opening paragraph and the lands covered in clauses (1)–(10) is restated to reflect an opinion of the Judge Advocate General of the Army (JAGR 1952/3179, 27 Mar. 1952). The exceptions to clause (10) make express the fact that the revised section does not cover certain easements authorized by earlier law. The word “over” includes the word “across”. The words “of the United States”, “and empowered”, “acquired lands”, “jurisdiction and”, and “municipality” are omitted as surplusage. The word “Commonwealth” is inserted to reflect the present status of Puerto Rico.In subsection (b), the words “for the easement” are substituted for the words “for the purpose for which granted”.In subsections (b) and (c), the word “easement” is substituted for the word “rights-of-way”.In subsection (c), the word “terminate” is substituted for the words “annulled and forfeited”. The words “and conditions” are omitted as covered by the word “terms”. The words “two-year period” are substituted for the words “a period of two consecutive years”. The words “of rights granted under authority hereof” are omitted as surplusage.

children.text: gas, water, sewer, and oil pipe lines;
substations for electric power transmission lines and pumping stations for gas, water, sewer, and oil pipe lines;
dams and reservoirs in connection with fish and wildlife programs, fish hatcheries, and other improvements relating to fish-culture;
poles and lines for the transmission or distribution of electric power;
poles and lines for the transmission or distribution of communications signals (including telephone and telegraph signals);
structures and facilities for the transmission, reception, and relay of such signals; and
any other purpose that the Secretary considers advisable.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Size of Easement
children.label: b .—Limitation on Size of Easement
children.text: No easement granted under this section may include more land than is necessary for the easement.
failure to comply with the terms of the grant;
nonuse for a two-year period; or

children.heading: .—Notice to Department of the Interior
children.label: d .—Notice to Department of the Interior
children.text: Copies of instruments granting easements over public lands under this section shall be furnished to the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: .—Disposition of Consideration
children.label: e .—Disposition of Consideration
children.text: Subsections (c) and (e) of shall apply with respect to in-kind consideration and proceeds received by the Secretary of a military department in connection with an easement granted under this section in the same manner as such subsections apply to in-kind consideration and money rentals received pursuant to leases entered into by that Secretary under such section.section 2667 of this title
